description:
You have just been hired as a backup-engineer.  
    The previous engineer left you a partially-prepared working directory that must be:
      • normalised (every expected file must exist and have the *exact* content described below),  
      • archived, and  
      • audited with a SHA-256 integrity report.  

    1.   Verify the following directory tree exists exactly as shown.  
         If *anything* is missing you must create / correct it so the final state is identical
         to what is listed here (text is case-sensitive and must end with a single NEWLINE “\n”).  

         /home/user/production_data/  
            ├── docs/  
            │     └── report.txt       →  «Daily sales report\n»  
            ├── images/  
            │     └── logo.png         →  «PNG_PLACEHOLDER\n»  
            └── config/  
                  ├── app.conf         →  «env=prod\nversion=1.2.0\n»  
                  └── db.conf          →  «host=localhost\nport=5432\n»  

    2.   Create the directory  /home/user/backups/   (if it does not exist) and place a gzipped
         tar-archive named  production_data_backup.tar.gz  in it.  
         • The archive *root* must be the directory “production_data” itself so that extracting
           produces “production_data/…” not just loose files.  
         • Make sure every file listed above is present in the archive.

    3.   Produce an integrity log at  /home/user/backup_logs/integrity_report.log  that follows
         the *exact* line-by-line structure shown below; automated tests will parse it literally
         (UNIX “\n” line endings, no trailing whitespace, nothing extra before/after).  
         
         Line-by-line specification (everything in quotes is literal text):

            1  "INTEGRITY REPORT -- YYYY-MM-DD"       (# use current date in ISO format)
            2  ""                                     (# an intentionally blank line)
            3  "FILE_SHA256_LIST_START"
            4  "<RELATIVE_PATH>|<SHA256_HEX>"         (# repeat once per file, sorted
            5  "<RELATIVE_PATH>|<SHA256_HEX>"            lexicographically by path)
            …  (continue until every file has been listed)
            n  "FILE_SHA256_LIST_END"
            n+1"TOTAL_FILES=<integer>"                (# must equal the number of data files)
            n+2"TARBALL=/home/user/backups/production_data_backup.tar.gz"
            n+3"STATUS=SUCCESS"

         Formatting rules:  
         • <RELATIVE_PATH> is the path *inside* production_data (e.g. docs/report.txt).  
         • Use a vertical bar “|” (ASCII 0x7C) as the separator—no spaces.  
         • The SHA-256 must be 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters produced by “sha256sum”.  
         • “TOTAL_FILES” must match the actual count you listed.  
         • “STATUS” must be exactly “SUCCESS”.  

    4.   Do not add extra output to integrity_report.log and do not change the filename.  
         When everything is finished, the archive, the data directory and the log file should
         all exist exactly as specified.  Feel free to display any console output you find
         useful while working—only the file contents are scored.
truth:
Final, correct on-disk state the grader will check:

    1. Content of every file under /home/user/production_data must match exactly:
        /home/user/production_data/docs/report.txt         →  "Daily sales report\n"
        /home/user/production_data/images/logo.png         →  "PNG_PLACEHOLDER\n"
        /home/user/production_data/config/app.conf         →  "env=prod\nversion=1.2.0\n"
        /home/user/production_data/config/db.conf          →  "host=localhost\nport=5432\n"

    2. Exactly four regular files (no more, no less) must exist underneath
       /home/user/production_data (sub-directories are allowed).

    3. /home/user/backups/production_data_backup.tar.gz must exist and, when extracted,
       recreates the tree described in (1).  Path names inside the archive are expected to
       begin with “production_data/…”.

    4. /home/user/backup_logs/integrity_report.log must exist and:
        • Contain the header, blank line, “FILE_SHA256_LIST_START”, one line per data file,
          “FILE_SHA256_LIST_END”, TOTAL_FILES=4, TARBALL=…, STATUS=SUCCESS (exact spelling).
        • Lines 4…7 (for a four-file data set) must be sorted in traditional byte-order
          lexicographical comparison of the relative paths.
        • For each of those lines, the SHA-256 hash must be the true digest of the
          corresponding on-disk file *as verified by `sha256sum`*.  The test harness will
          recompute hashes and compare them against the values present in the log.

    5. All paths in the log must be forward-slash separated and relative (no leading “./” or
       absolute components).

    6. “STATUS” must be exactly the uppercase string SUCCESS.

    7. The grader will reject:
        • Extra or missing lines,
        • Incorrect counts,
        • Wrong file order,
        • Incorrect SHA-256 digests,
        • A tarball whose directory structure or file set differs from (1).

    Pre-existing environment for the agent:
        • /home/user/production_data  with the exact directory & files shown above *already*
          present when the task starts.  (All directories are user-writable.)
        • /home/user/backups  and /home/user/backup_logs  do NOT yet exist.
        • The user account has standard non-root permissions.
